Ask whatever you need.

content: Okay, I was inferring you had a Gmail account here. If you have an exported mbox file, you can import it with these steps - install the importexport NG addon if not already done - highlight 'local folders' in the folder pane - rightclick and select the importexport option to import mbox file - locate and select your exported mbox file and press OK
creator: davidsk

content: Thanks, it's the "highlight 'localfolders' in the folder pane that stops me. I get no such option, no folders at all since I did not set up my gmail account. I don't want to handle my gmail with T-bird. I want to leave Gmail altogether. I'm just trying to use T-bird's UI to review my exported Google Takeout file of my Gmail account. It created an .mbox. T-bird doesn't give me any option to import the .mbox (only a zipped profile). Is it that T-bird will simply not let me do anything unless I set up an account? I guess I could set up another email account, if that is required, but I don't want to use the Gmail account I'm trying to leave.
creator: silvanet

content: That's correct. Thunderbird requires an ac count. However, you can view the mbox contents at https://www.mbox-viewer.com/
creator: davidsk

content: Thanks but I figured it out. I just had to create a Local Folders folder in the AppData\Roaming\thunderbird\Profiles\default\Mail\ folder ... so in AppData\Roaming\thunderbird\Profiles\***.default\Mail\Local Folders, then dropped the Google Takeout .mbox file in there, remove the .mbox extension, and reopen T-bird. Then all my email folders appeared in a Local Folder named All mail including spam and trash from my Takeout. Actually, I tried it in Betterbird and that worked perfectly. Sorry about that.
creator: silvanet

content: As far as I know the US Thunderbird version contains an en-US dictionary by default, which means unlike other locales it is part of the code and not an extension. I installed the en-Us version to test the spellcheck feature in a new profile. In fact I could reproduce a blocked or "hanging" compose window while correcting my deliberate spell errors in the spellcheck box, but most times I succeeded to close either the spellcheck box or the the compose window. It never completely froze Thunderbird. I never use the "Check spelling before sending" feature myself, preferring spellcheck as I type, so I never was confronted to this issue. If this issue is absent in troubleshoot mode, verify if one of your add-ons might interfere in spellchecking https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird
creator: Mapenzi

content: NO. Thunderbird is only your email client, your assistant. Your email provider is the one who authorizes the account, the password, the storage for messages. This is often the name to the right of the @ sign in an email address, but not always. It is NEVER thunderbirdd.

content:
I really have no idea what you are trying to achieve. You have installed Thunderbird and added a mail account that is I guess IMAP and it is now synching the email in the account as per the processes defined in the IMAP specification.

I would suggest you just wait until it is done as there is very little you can do while the account is brought into a synchronized state. Note that having an antivirus that scans incoming email wiull make the process slower and it may even cause it to time one and I have even heard of restarting. But that is an antivirus issue, nothing to do with Thunderbird.
creator: MattAuSupport
